metadata.description: A canonical vocabulary term — the normalization authority a catalog normalizes onto (the 'spec' half of the spec/reality pair; Capability is 'reality'). Formalizes entities/knowledge-family.md §4.2. A Knowledge-family entity: its lifecycle is curation (proposed -> under-review -> canonical -> deprecated), never realized by a provider. Cross-cutting foundational machinery — SHARED by multiple vocabularies that occupy DISJOINT subtrees under distinct roots (parent chains never cross roots): e.g. 'architecture-capability' (holds Capability [Knowledge], DAV's architecture-capability sense) and 'provider-capability' (the substrate provider-operation vocabulary; ADR-PROV-002). The `parent` chain up to a root is what keeps those vocabularies disjoint under one type.

term.description: Canonical term (single normalized handle) that items normalize onto. Unique within its root subtree.

definition.description: What the term means — the authoritative definition items are normalized against.

pillar.description: Top-level grouping the term rolls up to (e.g. a maturity-framework pillar).

domain.description: Term domain (prefix + name). For a provider-capability term this is the resource-type domain the capability operates over (the Category segment of a resource-type $id, e.g. Storage, Compute, Network); ADR-PROV-002.

root.description: The vocabulary root this term belongs to (e.g. 'architecture-capability', 'provider-capability'). Distinct roots are disjoint subtrees sharing this one type. Derivable by walking `parent` to the top; recorded for query convenience.

normalization_rules.description: How to normalize items for this subject matter onto the term.

category.description: Source grouping the term was drawn from (provenance of the term itself).

curation_state.description: Position in the curation lifecycle — the Knowledge-family interpretation of the four states. Seeded `canonical` from an authoritative taxonomy; `proposed` for a back-fill candidate a catalog surfaced. Promotion is a curation act applied by Policy, not by a provider.

term_handle.description: The canonical handle a term resolves to once curated to `canonical`. Other Knowledge entities (Capability.normalized_to, Alias.resolves_to) and — for provider-capability terms — provider capability declarations and Governance-Matrix policy targets bind to this via typed references rather than free-text.

relationships.description: parent — this term's parent in the hierarchy. The parent chain terminates at a vocabulary root and never crosses into another root's subtree (keeps shared-machinery vocabularies disjoint).
